bug: When you have a chat with a user and delete your account, an error occurs when the other user tries opening a chat with the deleted account.
When you have a chat open and click on the chat card from the chat list, the role-play ad data goes undefined.
When you repost more than 1 ad, the frontend UI appears to duplicate the ad more than once unless you refresh the page.

Logging in through Google on mobile causes the page to refresh and log you out immediately after logging in. This seems to be an issue with the Google login flow on mobile devices and is being investigated.

Markdown formatting in messages can sometimes render incorrectly, especially when using certain combinations of markdown syntax. We're working on improving the markdown parser to handle these cases more gracefully.

When you 'end a role-play' and then switch chats, it considers the other chats as 'ended' as well until you refresh the page. This is due to a state management issue where the 'ended' status is being applied globally instead of per chat. We're working on fixing this by ensuring that the 'ended' status is stored and managed on a per-chat basis.
When you sign out, then visit a URL that doesn't exist (i.e. you're on the Error 404 page), for some reason you're considered logged-in when you shouldn't be.
For some reason, if both users close a conversation with each other, the conversation doesn't get deleted

After you delete your account, sometimes for some reason, you're able to view your 'deleted profile'. Not always, but sometimes. This is likely due to a caching issue where the deleted profile data is still being stored in the cache and can be accessed until the cache is cleared or expires.
When a user edits/deletes their message, it's not reflected automatically to the other user for some reason.
The user's delete message is still shown as the latest message instead of 'deleted message
When you click on the chat card of the chat list you have open, the ad data goes undefined for some reason.
If you end a role-play and delete it and try to start a new role-play for the same ad, for some reason it's still shown as 'ended' until you refresh the page.
